claims.claim: Scaled Dot-Product Attention computes weights from QK^T / sqrt(d_k), applies softmax, then aggregates V.
Autoregressive decoding generates one token at a time, so each new prediction depends on previous tokens.
KV Cache stores previously computed Key and Value states for reuse during generation; it is not a cache of raw tokens or Query states.
KV Cache memory grows with sequence length and model/layer/head dimensions, making long-context inference a GPU memory and bandwidth problem.
RoPE does not invalidate KV Cache; it changes how position is applied to Q and K, so cached K states must remain aligned with the correct position convention.

As context length increases, attention has to compare against more historical states, and RoPE-style relative phase makes position handling part of the attention computation rather than a simple input-only decoration.
Sliding-window or local attention limits each token to a recent window, reducing computation and bounding cache growth for those layers, at the cost of weakening direct long-range access.
Sparse and block-sparse attention compute only selected token or block connections, such as local, global, or random links, to reduce the quadratic burden of full attention.
MQA and GQA reduce the number of Key/Value heads relative to Query heads, cutting KV Cache memory and memory bandwidth during decoding.
PagedAttention treats KV Cache memory like paged blocks, reducing fragmentation and enabling more efficient reuse in serving systems.
FlashAttention optimizes the attention computation and GPU memory IO; it is not the same layer of optimization as KV Cache, MQA, or GQA.

source_boundary_notes: 本集不声称 GPT、Claude 等闭源模型的具体位置编码或 KV Cache 实现。
RoPE 与 KV Cache 的关系必须表述为位置应用方式和位置偏移管理，而不是 RoPE 让 cache 失效。
Sliding Window、Sparse Attention、PagedAttention、MQA/GQA、FlashAttention 是不同工程层，不能合并成一个机制。
